# .ai Domain Investing 2026: Complete Beginner Guide

**The .ai domain extension has produced more seven-figure sales than any other country-code TLD in history. With Bot.ai selling for $1,200,000 in March 2026 and Lotus.ai closing at $400,000 just weeks earlier, the market is not slowing down. This guide covers everything a beginner needs to start investing in .ai domains with verified data, not hype.**

NameBuzz Research Team | Last updated March 2026

## Why .ai Domains Are the Hottest Investment in 2026

The numbers tell the story. .ai domain investing in 2026 is backed by a transaction record that no other alternative extension can match. Let's look at what is actually happening in the market right now.

### The Sales Record Speaks for Itself

The highest verified .ai domain sale of all time is AI.com at $70,000,000, a deal involving OpenAI and X Corp completed in 2023. That single transaction put the .ai extension on the map for investors worldwide.

But seven-figure sales are not limited to once-in-a-decade outliers. Consider the verified record from the [biggest .ai domain sales ever recorded](https://namebuzz.co/blog/biggest-ai-domain-sales-ever):

- X.ai: $5,000,000 (2017, Elon Musk/xAI)
- Data.ai: $1,800,000 (2022, App Annie rebrand)
- Voice.ai: $1,500,000 (2023)
- Chat.ai: $1,200,000 (2023)
- Bot.ai: $1,200,000 (March 4, 2026, Sedo)
- Work.ai: $1,050,000 (2023)
- Driver.ai: $1,000,657 (2023, GoDaddy Auctions)
- Security.ai: $1,000,000 (2023)

Eight domains at $1,000,000 or above. That kind of depth in the seven-figure range is rare for any extension outside of .com.

<!-- IMAGE: Bar chart comparing top .ai domain sales by price, alt="Bar chart of the top 10 highest .ai domain sales ranked by price" -->

### Why 2026 Is Different from 2023

In 2023, the .ai market was driven by a few massive deals at the top. What changed in 2024 and 2025 is that the mid-market filled in. Sales like send.ai at $859,164 (Afternic, 2024), draw.ai at $500,000 (Dan.com, 2024), and Wisdom.ai at $750,000 (2025) proved that demand extends well beyond a handful of trophy names.

Early 2026 has confirmed the trend. Bot.ai sold for $1,200,000 on Sedo on March 4. Lotus.ai closed at $400,000 on Brandforce on February 18. Speed.ai traded at $165,000 on January 22. Amber.ai sold for $115,000 on Spaceship the same day.

The buyer pool is expanding. The price floor for quality single-word .ai domains is rising. That is what makes .ai domain investing in 2026 a fundamentally different proposition than it was three years ago.

### The AI Industry Tailwind

Domain values track industry adoption. The artificial intelligence sector continues to attract hundreds of billions in annual investment. Every new AI startup, product, or service needs a web identity. The .ai extension gives them instant category signaling.

This is not speculation. It is observable in the data. Companies are paying premium prices for .ai domains because the extension communicates their core business in two characters.

## The Fundamentals: Asset vs. Liability

Before you spend a dollar, you need to understand the difference between a domain that is an asset and one that is a liability. Most beginners get this wrong.

### What Makes a Domain an Asset

An asset domain is one that appreciates in value or generates income. In the .ai space, asset domains share common traits:

- **Short length.** Single-word and two-word domains dominate the high-value sales. get.ai sold for $909,590. Cloud.ai sold for $600,000.
- **Generic keywords.** Industry terms that multiple buyers could want. Law.ai ($350,000), Security.ai ($1,000,000), and Voice.ai ($1,500,000) are all generic terms with broad commercial demand.
- **Commercial intent.** The word implies a product, service, or action. Work.ai ($1,050,000) and send.ai ($859,164) are action words tied to software categories.

<!-- IMAGE: Venn diagram showing the three qualities of asset-grade .ai domains: short length, generic keyword, and commercial intent, alt="Venn diagram of three key traits that make a .ai domain valuable" -->

### What Makes a Domain a Liability

A liability domain costs you money every year in renewal fees and has no realistic path to a sale. Warning signs include:

- Three-word or longer combinations
- Misspellings or unusual word pairings
- Narrow niche terms with only one or two potential buyers
- Trendy phrases that may not age well

Your renewal fee is the carrying cost of your investment. If you are paying $50 to $90 per year to hold a domain that nobody wants, that is a guaranteed loss. Multiply that across 50 or 100 domains and the numbers get painful fast.

### The Holding Period Reality

Domain investing is not day trading. The average hold time before a profitable sale can be one to five years or longer. You need to be prepared to carry renewal costs for every domain in your portfolio without a guaranteed exit date.

The investors who made money on Chat.ai, Voice.ai, and Bot.ai held those names through years of zero revenue before a buyer appeared. Patience is not optional in this market.

## How to Find Undervalued .ai Domains

Finding domains that are priced below their market value is the core skill of domain investing. Here is how to approach it systematically.

### Expiring Domain Lists

Every day, domains that were previously registered expire and become available for re-registration or drop into auction. Monitoring expiring .ai domain lists is one of the most reliable ways to pick up quality names at base registration cost.

Several services publish daily lists of expiring domains across all extensions. Filter these lists for .ai and review them manually. Automation helps with volume, but human judgment is essential for evaluating commercial potential.

### Aftermarket Bargains

Not every domain listed on the aftermarket is priced correctly. Sellers who need liquidity, who have lost interest, or who simply do not understand the value of their .ai names sometimes list them far below comparable sale prices.

Compare asking prices to verified sales data. If Adapt.ai sold for $300,000 in 2025, a similarly structured single-word .ai domain listed at $5,000 to $10,000 could represent significant upside. Always check the [verified .ai domain sales database](https://namebuzz.co) before making a buying decision.

<!-- IMAGE: Screenshot example of an aftermarket domain listing showing price comparison to verified sales, alt="Example aftermarket listing compared against verified .ai domain sale prices" -->

### Registration-Available Gems

The obvious single-word .ai domains were registered years ago. But new words enter the language constantly, especially in technology. Compound words, new product categories, and emerging industry terms can still be registered at base cost.

Check registrar availability regularly. When a new AI subcategory gains traction, the related .ai domain may still be open for standard registration. Speed matters here. Once a term hits mainstream awareness, the domain is gone.

### Watching Industry Trends

The best .ai domain investments align with where the AI industry is heading, not where it has already been. Study funding announcements, product launches, and regulatory developments. If a new AI application category is emerging, the related keyword domains are likely to appreciate.

Music.ai sold for $275,000 back in 2018, years before AI-generated music became mainstream. The buyer saw the trend early. That is the kind of thinking that produces outsized returns.

## Where to Buy .ai Domains

The marketplace you choose affects the domains available to you, the prices you pay, and the level of buyer protection you receive. Here are the major platforms for .ai domain transactions.

### Sedo

Sedo is one of the largest domain marketplaces globally and has facilitated some of the biggest .ai sales on record. Bot.ai sold for $1,200,000 on Sedo on March 4, 2026. Law.ai sold for $350,000 on Sedo in 2025.

Sedo offers both fixed-price listings and auction formats. Their escrow service handles payment and domain transfer, which reduces risk for both parties. Commission rates vary but typically run between 10% and 20% depending on the sale type.

### GoDaddy Auctions

GoDaddy operates one of the highest-traffic domain auction platforms. Driver.ai sold for $1,000,657 through GoDaddy Auctions in 2023. get.ai sold for $909,590 on the same platform. Cloud.ai sold for $600,000 through GoDaddy in 2025.

The advantage of GoDaddy is sheer buyer volume. More eyes on your listing means more potential bidders. GoDaddy also integrates aftermarket listings into its primary search, so end users looking to register a domain see aftermarket options automatically.

<!-- IMAGE: Comparison table of major .ai domain marketplaces showing fees, features, and notable sales, alt="Comparison table of Sedo, GoDaddy, Afternic, Dan.com, Brandforce, and Spaceship marketplace features" -->

### Afternic

Afternic, owned by GoDaddy, operates as a wholesale distribution network. send.ai sold for $859,164 on Afternic in 2024. Afternic listings appear across a network of partner registrars and resellers, which broadens exposure significantly.

For sellers, Afternic's distributed model means your domain gets listed on hundreds of partner sites automatically. For buyers, it means you may find .ai domains on smaller registrar websites that are actually sourced from Afternic's inventory.

### Dan.com

Dan.com specializes in a streamlined buy-now and installment payment model. draw.ai sold for $500,000 on Dan.com in 2024. The platform is known for its clean interface and buyer-friendly payment plans.

Installment payments are a notable feature. They allow buyers to spread payments over months, which can unlock sales that would not happen at a lump-sum price. For sellers, this means higher sell-through rates on mid-range domains.

### Brandforce

Brandforce is a newer entrant in the premium domain space. Lotus.ai sold for $400,000 on Brandforce on February 18, 2026. The platform focuses on curated premium domain sales and tends to attract brandable, high-quality names.

### Spaceship

Spaceship combines domain registration with aftermarket features. Amber.ai sold for $115,000 on Spaceship on January 22, 2026. The platform appeals to a younger, tech-savvy buyer demographic and offers competitive registration pricing.

### Choosing the Right Platform

No single marketplace is best for every situation. List high-value domains on multiple platforms simultaneously to maximize exposure. For buying, check all major marketplaces before committing to a purchase. Prices for the same domain can vary significantly across platforms.

## Valuation 101: How to Price a .ai Domain

<!-- VIDEO: native HTML5 video showing .ai domain investment strategy overview, poster="ai-domain-investment-guide-2026-video-thumb.jpg" -->

Pricing a .ai domain correctly is the difference between a profitable investment and dead money. Here is the framework professionals use.

### Comparable Sales Analysis

The most reliable valuation method is comparing your domain to verified sales of similar names. This is the same approach used in real estate appraisals. Find domains that match yours in structure, length, and keyword category, then use their sale prices as benchmarks.

For example, if you own a single generic English word .ai domain in the business software category, your comparables might include:

- Work.ai: $1,050,000
- send.ai: $859,164
- Adapt.ai: $300,000

Your valuation should fall within the range established by these comparable sales, adjusted for the relative strength of your specific keyword.

### The Four Valuation Factors

**1. Word length and type.** Single dictionary words command the highest prices. AI.com ($70,000,000), Voice.ai ($1,500,000), and Chat.ai ($1,200,000) are all single common English words. Two-word domains trade at a significant discount to single-word equivalents.

**2. Commercial intent.** Does the word describe a product, service, or action that businesses will pay for? Security.ai ($1,000,000) has obvious commercial intent. A word like "breeze" or "meadow" in .ai has far less direct commercial application.

**3. Search volume.** Keywords that people actively search for are worth more. Higher search volume means more type-in traffic and more potential buyers who want that exact keyword for their brand.

**4. Industry relevance.** Words directly tied to AI applications carry a premium. Data.ai ($1,800,000), Chat.ai ($1,200,000), and Bot.ai ($1,200,000) all describe core AI product categories.

<!-- IMAGE: Pyramid diagram showing the four valuation factors ranked by importance, alt="Pyramid ranking the four .ai domain valuation factors from most to least important" -->

### Using Valuation Tools

Manual comparable analysis is thorough but time-consuming. A [free .ai domain valuation tool](https://namebuzz.co/value) can automate the process by pulling from a database of verified transactions and applying the valuation factors algorithmically.

These tools are most useful as a starting point. They give you a data-backed range that you can then refine based on your knowledge of the specific keyword and current market conditions.

### Common Pricing Mistakes

**Overvaluing based on registration cost.** The fact that you paid $50 to register a domain does not make it worth $50,000. The market does not care what you paid. It cares about demand.

**Ignoring carrying costs.** If you hold a domain for five years at $75 per year in renewals, that is $375 in sunk cost. Factor that into your minimum acceptable sale price.

**Anchoring to the top sales.** AI.com sold for $70,000,000. Your three-word .ai domain is not comparable. Be honest about where your domain sits in the hierarchy.

## How to Sell a .ai Domain

Buying well is half the equation. The other half is selling effectively. Here is how to maximize your return.

### Setting the Right Price

Start with your comparable sales analysis. Price your domain within the range that the data supports. Overpricing leads to stale listings that buyers ignore. Underpricing leaves money on the table.

Consider listing with a "make offer" option rather than a fixed price. This lets the market tell you what buyers are willing to pay, which can sometimes exceed your expectations.

### Choosing Your Sales Channel

List on multiple platforms simultaneously. There is no exclusivity requirement on most marketplaces. A domain listed on Sedo, GoDaddy, Afternic, and Dan.com at the same time reaches the widest possible buyer pool.

For premium names (potential six-figure and above), consider working with a domain broker who specializes in high-value transactions. Brokers have direct relationships with end-user buyers that you may not be able to reach through marketplace listings alone.

<!-- IMAGE: Flowchart showing the decision process for choosing between marketplace listing and broker representation, alt="Decision flowchart for selling .ai domains through marketplaces versus brokers" -->

### Outbound Sales Strategy

Do not wait for buyers to find you. Identify companies and startups that could use your domain name. Research their current web presence. If they are operating on a longer or less memorable domain, your .ai name offers them a clear upgrade.

Reach out with a short, professional message that focuses on the value proposition for their business. Do not lead with price. Let them express interest first, then negotiate.

### Negotiation Basics

The buyer who contacts you first has already signaled demand. That gives you leverage. Key negotiation principles:

- Never accept the first offer without a counteroffer.
- Use verified comparable sales to justify your price.
- Be willing to walk away. Scarcity is your advantage.
- Consider installment payment plans to close deals that stall on price.

Speed.ai sold for $165,000 on January 22, 2026. Amber.ai sold for $115,000 on the same date. These mid-range sales show that there is active demand across a wide price spectrum, not just at the trophy level.

## Risks Every Investor Must Understand

Domain investing carries real risks. Ignoring them does not make them go away. Here is what can hurt you.

### Renewal Cost Drag

Every domain you hold costs money each year. If you build a portfolio of 100 .ai domains at $70 per year average renewal, that is $7,000 annually. Over five years with no sales, you have spent $35,000 just to hold your inventory.

This is the most common way beginners lose money. They register dozens of speculative names, fail to sell any, and the renewal costs compound into a significant loss. Be selective. Quality over quantity.

### Registry Policy Risk

The .ai extension is the country-code TLD for Anguilla, a British Overseas Territory. The Anguillan government controls the registry and can change policies, pricing, or eligibility requirements at any time.

While there is no indication of imminent policy changes, this structural risk is real. A sudden increase in registration fees or a restriction on who can register .ai domains could impact the market significantly.

<!-- IMAGE: Map showing Anguilla's location with callout explaining .ai registry governance, alt="Map highlighting Anguilla as the country that controls the .ai domain registry" -->

### Market Saturation

As .ai domain investing gains popularity, more investors enter the market. More investors means more competition for the best names and more supply of mid-tier names on the aftermarket. This can compress margins for average-quality domains.

The antidote is quality. Premium single-word .ai domains with strong commercial intent will hold value even in a crowded market. Generic multi-word domains are most vulnerable to saturation pressure.

### Liquidity Risk

Domains are illiquid assets. Unlike stocks, you cannot sell a domain instantly at market price. Finding the right buyer can take months or years. You may never find a buyer at all for some names.

Never invest money you cannot afford to lock up for an extended period. Domain investing should use discretionary capital only, never funds you need for living expenses or other obligations.

### Trademark Conflicts

Registering a domain that matches an existing trademark is a fast way to lose both the domain and money. Trademark holders can file UDRP (Uniform Domain-Name Dispute-Resolution Policy) complaints to seize domains registered in bad faith.

Before buying any .ai domain, search trademark databases to confirm the name is clear. This step is not optional. It is essential.

## Building Your First .ai Domain Portfolio

Starting with a clear strategy prevents the scattered, unfocused buying that ruins most beginners. Here is a step-by-step approach.

### Set a Budget and Stick to It

Decide how much you are willing to invest in your first year, including both acquisition costs and renewal fees. A reasonable starting budget for a beginner is $500 to $2,000. This allows you to acquire 5 to 15 domains at registration cost and cover first-year renewals.

Do not stretch your budget to chase expensive aftermarket names until you have experience evaluating domains and completing sales.

### Define Your Niche

Focus on one or two industry verticals that you understand well. If you work in healthcare, you will have better instincts about which medical AI terms have commercial value. If you know finance, you will spot fintech-related .ai opportunities that generalist investors miss.

Domain knowledge (the industry kind, not the URL kind) is a genuine competitive advantage in this market.

<!-- IMAGE: Example portfolio allocation showing domains distributed across 2 to 3 industry verticals, alt="Sample .ai domain portfolio allocation across healthcare, finance, and education verticals" -->

### The Barbell Strategy

Allocate your portfolio into two categories:

**Low-cost, high-potential registrations (80% of your budget).** These are domains you register at base cost that have a plausible path to a four-figure or five-figure sale. You will not hit on every one, but the cost of failure is just the annual renewal.

**Selective aftermarket acquisitions (20% of your budget).** These are domains you buy from other investors at $200 to $2,000 because you believe they are significantly undervalued based on comparable sales data. Higher cost, but higher conviction.

This barbell approach limits your downside while preserving upside potential.

### Track Everything

Maintain a spreadsheet of every domain you own. Track acquisition cost, renewal dates, renewal fees, listing status, inbound inquiries, and offers received. This data tells you which types of domains are generating interest and which are dead weight.

Review your portfolio quarterly. Drop domains that show no traction after 12 to 18 months. The renewal fee for a stale domain is better spent acquiring a new name with fresh potential.

### Learning from the Data

Study verified sales data regularly. The pattern in the numbers is clear. Look at the recent mid-range transactions:

- Cloud.ai: $600,000 (2025, GoDaddy)
- draw.ai: $500,000 (2024, Dan.com)
- Lotus.ai: $400,000 (2026, Brandforce)
- Law.ai: $350,000 (2025, Sedo)
- Adapt.ai: $300,000 (2025)

These are all single English words with clear meaning and commercial application. That is the profile you should target in your own acquisitions.

## Patterns in Premium .ai Domain Sales

Studying the 20 biggest .ai domain sales reveals consistent patterns that inform pricing, timing, and buyer behavior.

### Single-Word Domains Dominate

Every domain in the top 20 is a single word (or single character in the case of X.ai). No hyphenated names. No two-word combinations. No acronyms beyond "AI" itself. The market has spoken clearly: brevity commands premiums.

Of the 20 domains, 18 are common English words. The remaining two (X.ai and get.ai) are ultra-short at three characters or fewer. Word length matters, but word recognition matters more.

### 2023 Was the Inflection Year

Nine of the 20 biggest sales occurred in 2023. That single year produced more high-value .ai transactions than all previous years combined. The catalyst was clear: ChatGPT's launch in late 2022 triggered a wave of AI company formation, corporate rebranding, and speculative domain investment.

But the market did not collapse after 2023. Sales continued in 2024 (send.ai, draw.ai), 2025 (Wisdom.ai, Cloud.ai, Law.ai, Adapt.ai), and 2026 (Bot.ai, Lotus.ai, Speed.ai, Amber.ai). The pace slowed from the 2023 peak, but prices held firm.

### Price Tiers Are Emerging

The data reveals three distinct pricing tiers for premium .ai domains:

**Tier 1 ($1M+):** Category-defining keywords like AI, Chat, Voice, Security, Bot. Nine domains have reached this level.

**Tier 2 ($250K to $999K):** Strong single-word domains with clear industry relevance. Six domains fall here, including get.ai, send.ai, and Wisdom.ai.

**Tier 3 ($100K to $249K):** Brandable names and secondary keywords. Domains like Speed.ai and Amber.ai occupy this tier.

### Private Sales Still Lead

Of the 20 transactions, nine were private sales without public marketplace listings. These private deals account for over 93% of total dollar volume, driven almost entirely by the $70 million AI.com sale.

Among publicly listed sales, Sedo, GoDaddy Auctions, and Afternic are the most active venues. Newer platforms like Brandforce and Spaceship are beginning to capture share.

## What Drives Seven-Figure .ai Prices

Nine .ai domains have sold for $1 million or more. Understanding what pushed these specific names past the seven-figure mark helps investors identify which domains have similar potential.

### Exact Category Match

The highest-priced .ai domains match an entire product category. "Chat" is not just a keyword. It is a category. Same with "voice," "security," "data," and "bot." When a domain name equals a market segment, buyers will pay extraordinary premiums.

This is different from descriptive domains. "FastChat.ai" describes a product feature. "Chat.ai" defines a product category. The distinction is worth millions.

<!-- IMAGE: Comparison graphic showing category-match domains vs descriptive domains and their price differences, alt="Side-by-side comparison of category-match .ai domains versus descriptive .ai domains showing price premium" -->

### Buyer Intent Signals

Seven-figure .ai sales almost always involve corporate buyers, not individual speculators. OpenAI, xAI, App Annie. These are companies that needed a specific domain for a specific strategic purpose.

Corporate buyers evaluate domains differently than investors. They calculate the marketing spend a premium domain replaces, the brand equity it creates, and the competitive advantage it provides. Those calculations routinely produce seven-figure valuations for the right name.

### Scarcity at the Top

There is only one Chat.ai. Only one Voice.ai. Only one Security.ai. At the top of the .ai market, each domain is a unique asset with no direct substitute. You can find alternatives, but you cannot find equivalents.

This scarcity is absolute and permanent. Unlike physical assets that can be replicated, a domain name is a one-of-one digital property. That fundamental scarcity supports prices that might seem high by traditional domain standards.

### The .ai Extension Premium

The same word in .ai commands a higher price than in most other extensions (excluding .com). The reason is simple: .ai does double duty. It functions as both a web address and a brand signal. A company called "Voice.ai" does not need to explain that it works in artificial intelligence. The extension does that work automatically.

This built-in brand communication is worth a premium. It reduces marketing costs, accelerates brand recognition, and signals technical credibility to customers, investors, and partners.

## The Next Wave of High-Value .ai Sales

The sales data points to several trends that will shape the next round of premium .ai transactions.

### Vertical Keywords Are Rising

The biggest sales have historically gone to broad, horizontal keywords like "chat," "voice," and "work." But recent transactions like Law.ai ($350,000) suggest that vertical, industry-specific keywords are gaining value.

Expect domains like Health.ai, Finance.ai, Farm.ai, and similar vertical keywords to attract serious buyers as AI penetrates specific industries. These domains may not reach $1 million, but $300,000 to $750,000 is realistic for strong vertical matches.

### Brand Names Are Gaining Ground

Lotus.ai ($400,000) and Amber.ai ($115,000) broke from the keyword-first pattern. These are brand names, not descriptions. As the obvious keyword domains get acquired, buyers are turning to memorable, distinctive names that work as company identities.

This shift opens opportunities for domain investors holding brandable .ai names that are short, pronounceable, and visually clean.

### Q1 2026 Shows Sustained Demand

Four sales from early 2026 appear in the top 20: Bot.ai ($1,200,000), Lotus.ai ($400,000), Speed.ai ($165,000), and Amber.ai ($115,000). That is $1,880,000 in verified sales in less than three months.

The market is not slowing down. If anything, the entry of new marketplaces like Brandforce and Spaceship is bringing more liquidity and more buyers into the .ai domain space.

<!-- IMAGE: Line graph showing .ai domain sales volume by quarter from 2017 to Q1 2026, alt="Line graph tracking quarterly .ai domain sales volume from 2017 through Q1 2026" -->

### Price Floor Is Rising

The cheapest domain in our top 20 sold for $115,000. Two years ago, a domain at that price would not have made a top-20 list because fewer sales existed above it. Today, the floor for notable .ai sales keeps climbing as mid-tier domains change hands at higher prices.

For investors, this means the window to acquire premium .ai domains at five-figure prices is narrowing. Domains that seem expensive at $50,000 today may look like bargains compared to the next round of comparable sales.

## How to Check Your Own .ai Domain's Value

If you own a .ai domain, the sales data in this article gives you a framework for estimating its value. Here is how to approach a valuation.

### Compare Against Verified Sales

Start with the closest comparable sale. If you own a single-word .ai domain with a generic keyword, look at where it would rank in the table above. A word with similar search volume and commercial intent to "draw" or "adapt" might justify a valuation in the $300,000 to $500,000 range.

Two-word .ai domains, numeric .ai domains, and niche terms will fall below the ranges shown here. But they can still carry meaningful value in the right context.

### Use Automated Valuation Tools

Our [free .ai domain valuation tool](https://namebuzz.co/value) analyzes your domain against the full database of confirmed .ai sales. It factors in word length, keyword search volume, comparable transactions, and extension premium to generate a data-backed estimate.

Automated tools are a starting point, not a final answer. But they provide a useful baseline that you can refine with manual research.

### Factors That Increase Value

Based on the 20 biggest sales, these factors consistently correlate with higher prices:

- **Word length:** Shorter is better. Every domain in the top 20 is one word or fewer.
- **Keyword relevance:** Direct ties to AI applications (chat, voice, security) command the highest prices.
- **Brandability:** Even without keyword relevance, memorable names like Lotus and Amber perform well.
- **Search volume:** Words that people actually search for in combination with "AI" carry a measurable premium.
- **Buyer pool depth:** Domains relevant to multiple industries attract more potential buyers.

### Factors That Decrease Value

Not every .ai domain is valuable. Common pitfalls include:

- **Hyphenated domains:** No hyphenated .ai domain has ever sold for six figures in public records.
- **Long multi-word names:** Two or three-word combinations rarely command premium prices.
- **Misspellings or obscure terms:** The buyer pool shrinks dramatically for words that most people do not recognize.
- **Trademark conflicts:** Domains that match existing trademarks carry legal risk that reduces their market value.

# How Much Is a .ai Domain Worth in 2026?

**The value of a .ai domain ranges from under $1,000 for unregistered names to $70,000,000 for the right premium keyword. Verified 2023 to 2026 sales data shows single-word .ai domains routinely selling for six and seven figures, driven by explosive demand from AI startups, enterprise buyers, and investors betting on artificial intelligence branding.**

NameBuzz Research Team | Last updated March 2026

<!-- IMAGE: Hero image showing a .ai domain name on a price tag with ascending value chart in the background, alt="How much is a .ai domain worth in 2026 showing rising domain values" -->

## Table of Contents

- [What Determines .ai Domain Value](#what-determines-ai-domain-value)
- [Verified .ai Domain Sales Data](#verified-ai-domain-sales-data)
- [How to Estimate Your .ai Domain's Worth](#how-to-estimate-your-ai-domains-worth)
- [Price Ranges by Domain Type](#price-ranges-by-domain-type)
- [Where the Market Is Heading in 2026](#where-the-market-is-heading-in-2026)
- [How to Sell Your .ai Domain](#how-to-sell-your-ai-domain)
- [Common Valuation Mistakes to Avoid](#common-valuation-mistakes-to-avoid)
- [Frequently Asked Questions](#frequently-asked-questions)
- [Sources and Methodology](#sources-and-methodology)

## What Determines .ai Domain Value

Not all .ai domains are created equal. A four-letter random string might fetch $500, while a single dictionary word can command seven figures. Understanding the factors that separate a $1,000 domain from a $1,000,000 domain is the first step toward pricing yours accurately.

### Word Length and Type

Short domains sell for more. That pattern holds across every TLD, and .ai is no exception. Single-word, dictionary-term .ai domains dominate the top of the sales charts. Look at the data: AI.com ($70,000,000), Data.ai ($1,800,000), Voice.ai ($1,500,000), Chat.ai ($1,200,000), Bot.ai ($1,200,000).

Every one of those is a single common English word. Two-word combinations, acronyms, and brandable invented words trade at lower multiples. The shorter and more recognizable the word, the higher the price ceiling.

### Keyword Search Volume and Commercial Intent

A .ai domain's value correlates directly with how many people search for that keyword in connection with AI products or services. "Security" has massive search volume and clear commercial intent. That is why Security.ai sold for $1,000,000.

Compare that to a word like "Amber," which is a proper noun with lower direct AI commercial intent. Amber.ai sold for $115,000. Still a strong sale, but the gap reflects the difference in keyword demand.

### Industry Relevance to AI

Domains that map directly to AI use cases carry a premium. Words like "chat," "bot," "voice," "data," and "cloud" describe core AI product categories. Buyers pay more because the domain itself communicates what the product does.

Domains with indirect AI relevance, like "Lotus" or "Driver," still sell well when paired with .ai, but they depend more on the buyer having a specific brand vision. Lotus.ai sold for $400,000 in February 2026, and Driver.ai brought $1,000,657 in 2023.

<!-- IMAGE: Infographic showing the three tiers of .ai domain value factors with word length, search volume, and industry relevance as pillars, alt="Three key factors that determine .ai domain value infographic" -->

### Brandability and Memorability

A domain that is easy to spell, easy to say, and easy to remember commands a premium. This is why single-syllable words perform so well. "Get," "send," "draw," and "speed" are all punchy, action-oriented words that stick in a buyer's mind.

Get.ai sold for $909,590. Send.ai sold for $859,164. Draw.ai sold for $500,000. Speed.ai sold for $165,000. These are all verb-based, immediately understood, and easy to brand around.

### Comparable Sales History

The best predictor of what your .ai domain is worth is what similar domains have actually sold for. Abstract valuation models break down quickly without real transaction data. That is why we built a [verified .ai domain sales database](https://namebuzz.co) tracking 500+ confirmed .ai transactions.

Comparable sales give you a defensible price range. If you own a single-word .ai domain in a vertical where similar words sold for $300,000 to $750,000, that range becomes your pricing anchor.

## Verified .ai Domain Sales Data

Raw numbers tell the story better than speculation. Below are 20 verified .ai domain sales, sourced from public auction records, registrar disclosures, and direct reporting.

<!-- IMAGE: Bar chart visualization of the top 20 .ai domain sales sorted by price, alt="Chart showing top 20 verified .ai domain sales from 2017 to 2026" -->

### What the Data Reveals

Several patterns stand out when you examine these 20 sales together.

First, eight of the top ten sales are single dictionary words with direct technology or AI relevance. The outlier, Driver.ai, still describes a clear product category (autonomous driving).

Second, prices cluster in distinct bands. There is a clear gap between the $1,000,000+ tier and the $100,000 to $500,000 tier. Very few domains land in between, which suggests the market values category-defining words on a different scale than strong-but-niche terms.

Third, the sales are accelerating. Five of the top 20 sales happened in 2025 or early 2026. Bot.ai sold for $1,200,000 in March 2026. Lotus.ai sold for $400,000 in February 2026. The market is not cooling down.

### Private Sales vs. Public Auctions

Roughly half of the sales in our dataset came through private transactions. This matters because private sales often involve direct negotiation between buyer and seller, with no public listing.

Public auction sales through platforms like GoDaddy Auctions, Sedo, and Afternic provide transparent pricing but may reflect lower prices than private deals for the same quality of domain. Driver.ai sold at GoDaddy Auctions for $1,000,657, while comparable domains like Work.ai and Security.ai sold privately for similar amounts.

<!-- IMAGE: Pie chart comparing private sales vs public auction sales for .ai domains, alt="Comparison of private versus public auction .ai domain sales distribution" -->

## How to Estimate Your .ai Domain's Worth

Valuing a domain is part science, part market awareness. Here is a practical framework for estimating what your .ai domain could sell for.

### Step 1: Find Comparable Sales

Start with domains that match yours in structure and category. If you own a single-word .ai domain in the legal space, Law.ai at $350,000 is your closest comparable. If you own a verb-based action word, look at get.ai ($909,590), send.ai ($859,164), and draw.ai ($500,000).

The [free .ai domain valuation tool](https://namebuzz.co/value) at NameBuzz pulls comparable sales automatically and gives you a data-backed estimate in seconds.

### Step 2: Assess Keyword Strength

Check your domain's primary keyword in Google Keyword Planner or a similar tool. Look at monthly search volume, cost-per-click for related ads, and the number of companies competing for that keyword.

A word with 50,000+ monthly searches and $5+ CPC is going to be worth significantly more than a word with 1,000 monthly searches and $0.50 CPC. The advertising economics alone justify a higher domain price for high-CPC keywords.

### Step 3: Evaluate Industry Demand

Which industry does your domain serve? AI-native categories like chatbots, voice AI, data analytics, cloud computing, and cybersecurity command the highest premiums. Look at the sales data: Chat.ai, Voice.ai, Data.ai, Cloud.ai, and Security.ai all sold for $600,000 or more.

Adjacent industries like law (Law.ai at $350,000), music (music.ai at $275,000), and logistics (Driver.ai at $1,000,657) also show strong demand, but typically at lower multiples unless the word has crossover appeal.

<!-- IMAGE: Flowchart showing the three-step .ai domain valuation process from comparable sales to keyword strength to industry demand, alt="Step-by-step process for estimating .ai domain value" -->

### Step 4: Factor in Domain Length

Count the characters. Four-letter .ai domains are in a different category than eight-letter domains. Here is a rough breakdown based on verified sales:

- 2 to 4 characters: Highest value tier. Examples include X.ai ($5,000,000) and get.ai ($909,590).
- 5 to 6 characters: Strong value. Examples include Voice.ai ($1,500,000), Cloud.ai ($600,000), Speed.ai ($165,000).
- 7+ characters: Value depends heavily on keyword strength. Examples include Security.ai ($1,000,000), Wisdom.ai ($750,000), Driver.ai ($1,000,657).

Longer words can still command premium prices if the keyword is strong enough. Security.ai proves that a nine-letter domain can sell for $1,000,000 when the word matches a massive industry.

### Step 5: Consider Timing

The .ai domain market has trended upward consistently since 2022. Bot.ai selling for $1,200,000 in March 2026 and Lotus.ai selling for $400,000 in February 2026 show that buyer demand remains strong.

Selling now, while AI investment is near peak levels, may yield better results than holding indefinitely. Markets cycle, and the window of maximum demand does not stay open forever.

## Price Ranges by Domain Type

Based on verified sales and market activity, here are realistic price ranges for different categories of .ai domains in 2026.

<!-- IMAGE: Tiered pricing table showing .ai domain value ranges by domain type from premium single-word to brandable names, alt="Price range tiers for different types of .ai domains in 2026" -->

### Premium Single-Word Domains ($500,000 to $5,000,000+)

These are category-defining dictionary words with high search volume and direct AI relevance. Think nouns and verbs that describe entire product categories: data, voice, chat, bot, work, security, cloud.

Only a few hundred domains fall into this tier. If you own one, you are holding a high-value asset. Recent sales in this range include Bot.ai ($1,200,000 in March 2026) and Wisdom.ai ($750,000 in 2025).

### Strong Single-Word Domains ($100,000 to $500,000)

Solid dictionary words with clear meaning but slightly less direct AI application. These are brandable, memorable, and useful for startups that want an instant credibility boost.

Examples from verified sales: Lotus.ai ($400,000), Law.ai ($350,000), Adapt.ai ($300,000), music.ai ($275,000), Speed.ai ($165,000), Amber.ai ($115,000).

### Mid-Tier Domains ($10,000 to $100,000)

Two-word combinations, less common single words, and strong acronyms. These domains are useful for specific business applications but lack the broad category appeal of top-tier names.

Most working professionals who own a decent .ai domain fall into this range. The key to maximizing value here is patience and finding the right buyer who needs your specific word.

### Entry-Level Domains ($500 to $10,000)

Three-word combinations, longer phrases, misspellings, and niche terms. These are the most common .ai domains on the market. They can still sell, especially if they match a specific product name or startup idea, but do not expect life-changing returns.

### Registration-Cost Domains (Under $500)

Some .ai domains are only worth their annual registration fee. If the word has no search volume, no commercial intent, and no brandability, it may not be worth renewing.

<!-- VIDEO: native HTML5 video showing how to use the NameBuzz valuation tool to estimate a .ai domain's worth with live comparable sales data, poster="how-much-is-my-ai-domain-worth-video-thumb.jpg" -->

## Where the Market Is Heading in 2026

The .ai domain market is not slowing down. Here is what the data tells us about where prices are headed.

### Early 2026 Sales Signal Continued Strength

Three notable sales have already closed in the first quarter of 2026. Bot.ai sold for $1,200,000 on March 4 via Sedo. Lotus.ai sold for $400,000 on February 18 via Brandforce. Speed.ai ($165,000) and Amber.ai ($115,000) both sold on January 22.

These transactions show that buyers are still willing to pay premium prices. The $1,200,000 Bot.ai sale is particularly significant because it matches the 2023 sale price of Chat.ai, suggesting the market has sustained its highs rather than retreating.

### Enterprise Adoption Is Driving Demand

Large companies are buying .ai domains for rebranding and product launches. App Annie's acquisition of Data.ai for $1,800,000 in 2022 set the template. When a company rebrands around an AI-forward identity, a premium .ai domain becomes a strategic asset, not just a marketing expense.

This trend has only intensified as more enterprises announce AI-focused products and divisions. Every Fortune 500 company with an AI strategy is a potential buyer.

<!-- IMAGE: Timeline graphic showing major .ai domain sales from 2017 to 2026 with price points plotted on an upward curve, alt="Timeline of major .ai domain sales showing price growth from 2017 to 2026" -->

### Supply Constraints Support Higher Prices

Unlike .com, where billions of combinations are available, .ai has a much smaller pool of short, meaningful words. Most single-word dictionary terms in .ai are already registered. This supply constraint means the best domains can only change hands through resale, which supports higher pricing.

New registrations are still available for longer phrases and invented words, but the premium inventory is locked up. Scarcity is a feature of this market, not a bug.

### Risks to Watch

No market goes straight up forever. The .ai domain market faces several potential headwinds.

If AI investment slows due to economic conditions or regulatory pressure, demand for .ai domains could soften. A major shift in naming conventions, away from .ai toward other extensions, could also reduce demand. And speculative holders flooding the market with overpriced listings could create the perception of a bubble, even if underlying fundamentals remain strong.

Realistic sellers price based on comparable data, not hopes. That is the best protection against market shifts.

## How to Sell Your .ai Domain

Owning a valuable .ai domain is only half the equation. You also need to sell it effectively. Here is a practical guide.

### Choose Your Sales Channel

You have three main options.

**Public Marketplaces.** Sedo, Afternic, Dan.com, and GoDaddy Auctions are the most active platforms for .ai domain sales. Bot.ai sold on Sedo. Driver.ai and get.ai sold on GoDaddy Auctions. Send.ai sold on Afternic. Draw.ai sold on Dan.com.

These platforms handle escrow, transfer, and payment processing. Fees typically range from 10% to 20% of the sale price.

**Domain Brokers.** For domains you believe are worth $100,000 or more, a broker can access buyers you cannot reach on your own. Many of the private sales in our dataset, including Voice.ai, Chat.ai, and Work.ai, likely involved brokers.

Broker commissions typically range from 10% to 15%, but they can justify the fee by finding buyers willing to pay top dollar.

**Direct Outreach.** If you know which companies would benefit from your domain, reach out directly. This approach works best when your domain matches a company's product name or a category they are actively building in.

Direct sales avoid marketplace fees, but require more effort and negotiation skill.

<!-- IMAGE: Comparison table showing pros and cons of three sales channels for selling .ai domains including marketplaces, brokers, and direct outreach, alt="Comparison of .ai domain sales channels showing marketplaces brokers and direct outreach" -->

### Set a Defensible Price

Base your asking price on comparable sales, not gut feeling. If similar domains have sold for $200,000 to $400,000, pricing yours at $2,000,000 will generate zero interest. Pricing it at $300,000 to $500,000 gives buyers a reason to engage.

Use the sales table in this article and our [free .ai domain valuation tool](https://namebuzz.co/value) to find your range. Then set your asking price at the top of that range to leave room for negotiation.

### Optimize Your Landing Page

If your domain resolves to a parked page, make sure it clearly communicates that the domain is for sale. Include a contact method, a professional design, and your asking price or a "make an offer" prompt.

Serious buyers judge credibility quickly. A well-designed sales landing page signals that you are a professional seller, not someone hoping to win the lottery.

### Be Ready to Negotiate

Most domain sales involve negotiation. Buyers will almost always counter with a lower offer. Prepare a walk-away price before you start, and be willing to hold firm if the offer falls below your comparable-sales-backed minimum.

Response time matters too. Buyers who inquire today may lose interest next week. Respond within 24 hours to keep deals moving.

### Understand Transfer and Payment

Use escrow for every transaction over $1,000. Platforms like Sedo and Dan.com have built-in escrow. For direct sales, Escrow.com is the industry standard.

The .ai domain transfer process involves updating the registrar records to the buyer's account. Processing typically takes 1 to 5 business days. Make sure your domain is unlocked and your auth code is ready before closing the deal.

## Common Valuation Mistakes to Avoid

Overvaluing or undervaluing your domain costs money either way. Here are the most common mistakes we see.

### Anchoring on the AI.com Sale

AI.com sold for $70,000,000. That does not mean your .ai domain is worth millions. AI.com is a once-in-a-generation domain that perfectly matches the most searched technology term in the world. It is an outlier, not a benchmark.

Base your valuation on sales of domains similar to yours in length, keyword strength, and industry relevance. For most single-word .ai domains, the realistic range is $100,000 to $1,000,000.

<!-- IMAGE: Warning icon graphic illustrating common domain valuation mistakes sellers should avoid, alt="Common .ai domain valuation mistakes to avoid when pricing your domain" -->

### Ignoring Comparable Sales

Some sellers price based on what they think their domain "should" be worth, ignoring what similar domains have actually sold for. This is the fastest way to sit on an unsold domain for years.

The verified sales data exists. Use it. If your domain is comparable to Adapt.ai ($300,000) and Law.ai ($350,000), that is your range. Wishing it were worth $2,000,000 does not make it so.

### Overvaluing Length and Complexity

Two-word .ai domains are not worthless, but they are worth significantly less than single-word equivalents. A domain like "SmartDataAnalytics.ai" is not going to fetch the same price as Data.ai. Accept the market reality and price accordingly.

### Undervaluing Timing

Holding a domain indefinitely is not a free strategy. Registration renewals cost money every year. More importantly, the AI hype cycle will not last forever at current intensity. Selling into a strong market, like the one we are in now, often yields better results than waiting for an imaginary perfect moment.

### Not Using Data Tools

Manual valuation based on intuition leads to poor pricing. Automated tools that pull from verified sales databases provide faster, more accurate estimates. The [free .ai domain valuation tool](https://namebuzz.co/value) at NameBuzz uses 500+ confirmed transactions to generate data-backed estimates.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is the most expensive .ai domain ever sold?

AI.com sold for $70,000,000 in 2023 in a private transaction involving OpenAI and X Corp. This is the highest confirmed .ai domain sale by a significant margin. The second-highest sale is X.ai at $5,000,000, purchased by Elon Musk in 2017 for his xAI venture. The gap between first and second place underscores how exceptional the AI.com transaction was.

### How much does an average .ai domain sell for?

Most .ai domains that actually sell (as opposed to sitting unsold on marketplaces) trade between $500 and $25,000. Premium single-word .ai domains with strong keyword relevance regularly sell for $100,000 to $1,000,000 or more. The 20 verified sales in our dataset range from $115,000 (Amber.ai) to $70,000,000 (AI.com), but these represent the top tier of the market, not the average.

### Where can I sell my .ai domain?

The most active platforms for .ai domain sales are Sedo, Afternic, Dan.com, and GoDaddy Auctions. Each has handled confirmed six-figure and seven-figure .ai transactions. For domains you believe are worth $100,000 or more, consider working with a domain broker who specializes in premium sales. Private sales through direct outreach are another option, especially if you can identify companies that would benefit from your specific domain.

### What makes a .ai domain valuable?

Five factors drive .ai domain value: word length (shorter is better), keyword search volume (higher is better), industry relevance to AI (direct relevance commands premiums), brandability (easy to spell, say, and remember), and comparable sales history. Single dictionary words tied to AI product categories like "chat," "bot," "voice," "data," and "security" command the highest prices, as shown by verified sales ranging from $1,000,000 to $5,000,000 for these types of domains.

### Are .ai domains a good investment in 2026?

Verified sales data shows consistent price growth in the .ai domain market from 2022 through early 2026. Bot.ai sold for $1,200,000 in March 2026, matching the 2023 sale price of Chat.ai. Lotus.ai sold for $400,000 in February 2026. These recent transactions show the market has maintained its highs. However, investing in domains carries risk, and most .ai domains are not worth six or seven figures. The best returns come from short, single-word domains with clear AI relevance and strong keyword demand.

### How long does it take to sell a .ai domain?

Sale timelines vary widely. Premium domains with strong inbound interest can sell in days or weeks. Mid-tier domains often take 3 to 12 months to find the right buyer. Lower-value domains may take years or never sell at all. Pricing your domain competitively based on comparable sales, listing on multiple platforms, and being responsive to inquiries all reduce time to sale.

<!-- IMAGE: FAQ section header graphic with question mark icons and .ai domain theme, alt="Frequently asked questions about .ai domain valuation and pricing" -->

## Sources and Methodology

The sales data in this article comes from the following sources, cross-referenced for accuracy.

### Primary Data Sources

**DN Journal.** DN Journal is one of the longest-running domain sales reporting outlets, tracking verified transactions across all major TLDs including .ai. Their weekly and monthly sales reports provide confirmed prices from public auctions and reported private sales.

**Sedo.** Sedo is one of the largest domain marketplaces globally, handling transactions including Bot.ai ($1,200,000 in March 2026) and Law.ai ($350,000 in 2025). Their publicly reported sales data is verified through their escrow and transfer process.

**Afternic.** Afternic, a GoDaddy-owned marketplace, processes high-volume domain sales including send.ai ($859,164 in 2024). Their sales data is cross-listed with GoDaddy's reporting infrastructure.

**NameBio.** NameBio maintains one of the most comprehensive historical domain sales databases, aggregating data from dozens of auction platforms, registrars, and marketplaces. Their records include transaction dates, prices, and sale venues.

**GoDaddy.** GoDaddy Auctions and the GoDaddy marketplace have facilitated several major .ai sales including Driver.ai ($1,000,657 in 2023), get.ai ($909,590 in 2023), and Cloud.ai ($600,000 in 2025). Their auction data is publicly accessible and timestamped.

### Methodology

All 20 sales listed in this article have been verified through at least one of the sources above. Private sales are included only when the transaction has been confirmed through registrar WHOIS changes, direct reporting by buyer or seller, or coverage in established domain industry publications.

We exclude unverified claims, rumored sales, and transactions where the price cannot be independently confirmed. Our full database of 500+ .ai domain sales at [namebuzz.co](https://namebuzz.co) follows the same verification standard.

Prices are listed in US dollars. Where a sale was reported in another currency, we used the exchange rate on the transaction date for conversion.

<!-- IMAGE: Methodology diagram showing how NameBuzz verifies domain sales data from multiple sources, alt="NameBuzz domain sales verification methodology showing data sources and cross-referencing process" -->

## Conclusion

The .ai domain market is real, active, and data-rich. If you own a .ai domain and want to know how much is a .ai domain worth, start with the verified sales data in this article. Find domains comparable to yours in length, keyword strength, and industry relevance. Use those sales as your pricing anchor.

The data is clear on what drives value: short words win, AI-relevant keywords command premiums, and category-defining terms sell for seven figures. Bot.ai selling for $1,200,000 in March 2026 proves this market is not slowing down.

If you want a faster answer, use our [free .ai domain valuation tool](https://namebuzz.co/value) to get a data-backed estimate based on 500+ verified transactions. Price with data, not hope. Sell into strength, not speculation. And always verify your comparables before setting an asking price.

The window for maximum .ai domain demand is open right now. Whether you sell today or hold for a better offer, make that decision based on the numbers, not on gut feeling.

## The stt.ai Sale — Full Breakdown

The stt.ai domain sale is one of the more notable .ai transactions reported in 2026. At $74,456 via a 24-month lease-to-own arrangement, it represents a significant commitment to a short .ai domain — and an increasingly popular deal structure in the premium domain market.

Here are the confirmed details.

**Domain:** stt.ai

**Sale Price:** $74,456 (24-month lease-to-own)

**Venue:** Atom.com, reported by DN Journal

**Seller:** James Booth, DomainBooth.com

**Year:** 2026

**Domain Type:** 3-letter .ai (country-code top-level domain, Anguilla)

**Deal Structure:** Lease-to-own (LTO) — the buyer pays over a 24-month period, with ownership transferring upon completion of all payments.

DN Journal is one of the domain industry's longest-running trade publications. Founded by Ron Jackson in 2003, it has tracked and reported domain sales for over two decades. When DN Journal reports a sale price, the industry treats it as verified.

The $74,456 price point places stt.ai among the notable 3-letter .ai sales on record. While it did not reach six figures, the LTO structure means the buyer committed to this amount over two years — a signal of serious intent and commercial use rather than speculative flipping.

### What Makes This Deal Notable

Three-letter domains occupy a specific tier in the .ai market. They are short enough to be highly brandable, easy to type, and easy to remember. But they lack the instant keyword recognition of full-word domains like voice.ai or chat.ai.

The LTO deal structure is worth noting. Lease-to-own arrangements have become increasingly common for premium domains, allowing buyers to spread payments over time while securing the domain immediately. For the seller, LTOs can attract buyers who might not have the full purchase price upfront, broadening the market. The $74,456 total over 24 months works out to roughly $3,102 per month — a manageable expense for a funded startup or growth-stage company.

In stt.ai's case, the domain's value is driven by a direct connection to one of the most commercially important AI technologies: speech-to-text.

<!-- IMAGE: Infographic showing the stt.ai sale details including price, venue, and domain characteristics, alt="stt.ai domain sale details infographic showing $74,456 LTO price and key attributes" -->

### The Buyer — What We Know

The buyer of stt.ai has not been publicly identified. This is standard for domain transactions reported through trade publications. DN Journal reports the sale price and venue but does not always disclose buyer identities unless they are part of the public record.

The seller, James Booth of DomainBooth.com, is a known domain investor. The deal was brokered through Atom.com, one of the domain industry's established sales platforms.

What we can infer is that the buyer likely operates in the speech-to-text, voice AI, or audio technology space. A $74,456 domain commitment over 24 months is a serious investment, typically made by funded startups or enterprise companies building a brand around a specific technology vertical.

No company currently operating under the stt.ai brand has been publicly announced as of this writing.

## Why 3-Letter .ai Domains Command Premium Prices

The .ai extension has become the default namespace for artificial intelligence companies. But within that extension, domain length is one of the strongest predictors of value.

### The Length Premium

Shorter domains sell for more. This pattern holds across every top-level domain, but it is especially pronounced in .ai because the extension itself is only two characters. A domain like stt.ai is just six characters total — five letters and a dot. That creates a URL that is extraordinarily compact.

Consider the difference in branding between these two options:

- **stt.ai** — Clean, memorable, easy to say aloud, fits on a business card
- **speechtotextplatform.ai** — Descriptive but long, hard to remember, no brand identity

Investors and companies consistently pay premiums for shorter domains because they convert better. Users are more likely to type short domains directly, remember them after seeing them once, and trust them as legitimate brands.

### The Scarcity Factor

There are only 17,576 possible 3-letter combinations using the 26 letters of the English alphabet. That is the total supply of 3-letter .ai domains that can ever exist.

Not all of those are registered. Not all registered ones are for sale. And not all that are for sale carry keyword relevance. The pool of genuinely valuable 3-letter .ai domains is far smaller than 17,576.

This scarcity drives prices upward year over year. As more AI companies launch, the supply of desirable short .ai domains shrinks while demand grows. Basic economics dictates the result.

<!-- IMAGE: Supply and demand chart showing limited 3-letter .ai domain supply vs growing AI company demand, alt="Chart showing finite supply of 17,576 possible 3-letter .ai domains versus rising demand from AI companies" -->

### Letter Combinations Matter

Not all 3-letter domains are equal. The value depends on several factors.

**Pronounceability.** Domains that can be spoken as a word or clear acronym are worth more. "STT" is easily said as three distinct letters and is a recognized abbreviation.

**Keyword association.** Letters that map to known terms command higher prices. STT maps to speech-to-text, a well-known technology category. Compare that to a random combination like "qzx" which maps to nothing.

**Industry relevance.** In the .ai extension, letter combinations that connect to AI-related technologies or business terms carry a premium. STT's connection to speech-to-text makes it a natural fit for the .ai namespace.

**Existing usage.** If an abbreviation is already widely used in technical documentation, industry conversations, or product descriptions, the matching domain benefits from that pre-existing awareness.

**Speech-to-text is a billion-dollar market.** The global speech and voice recognition market was valued at over $12 billion in 2025 and is projected to exceed $50 billion by 2032. A domain that maps directly to this market carries proportional value.

**STT is a standard abbreviation.** Unlike made-up acronyms, STT is used in technical documentation by Google, Amazon, Microsoft, and every major cloud provider. When developers search for speech-to-text solutions, they frequently use the abbreviation STT.

**The .ai extension reinforces the keyword.** Speech-to-text is an AI technology. Putting it on the .ai extension creates a domain where every component — the letters and the extension — communicates the same message. That alignment is rare and valuable.

**Compact URL.** At six total characters, stt.ai is one of the shortest possible domain-plus-extension combinations. Short URLs have measurable advantages in click-through rates, brand recall, and social media sharing.

## What STT Could Stand For — Likely Buyers and Use Cases

The acronym STT has multiple interpretations, which broadens the potential buyer pool. Here are the most likely use cases for stt.ai.

### Speech-to-Text (Most Likely)

Speech-to-text is the standout interpretation. STT is the standard abbreviation used across the AI and software industries for automatic speech recognition technology that converts spoken language into written text.

**Market size:** The speech-to-text market alone is projected to surpass $10 billion by 2028.

**Key players:** Google Cloud STT, Amazon Transcribe, Microsoft Azure Speech, OpenAI Whisper, Deepgram, AssemblyAI, and dozens of startups.

**Use cases:** Real-time transcription, meeting notes, accessibility tools, call center analytics, subtitle generation, voice search, dictation software, and medical documentation.

A company operating in any of these segments would find stt.ai to be a near-perfect brand domain. It tells users exactly what the product does, it is easy to remember, and it ranks naturally for relevant search queries.

### Security Token Trading

In finance, STT can stand for security token trading — the buying and selling of blockchain-based security tokens that represent ownership in real-world assets.

The security token market is growing as regulatory frameworks mature. A platform operating at the intersection of AI and security token trading could use stt.ai to brand an AI-powered trading platform.

### Stock Ticker / Trading Technology

STT is also the NYSE stock ticker for State Street Corporation, a major financial services company. While State Street itself is unlikely to need stt.ai for its primary brand, the association with financial services adds value for any fintech AI company.

### Generic 3-Letter Brand

Some buyers purchase 3-letter domains purely for brandability, without a specific acronym in mind. Three letters are short enough to create a brand from scratch. A company could adopt STT as its name and build brand meaning around it, similar to how companies like IBM or SAP turned abbreviations into globally recognized brands.

The stt.ai sale is more than a single transaction. It is a data point that tells us something about where the .ai domain market stands in 2026.

### LTO Deals Are Expanding the Market

The lease-to-own structure of the stt.ai deal reflects a broader trend in the domain market. LTOs lower the barrier to entry for buyers who want premium domains but prefer to spread payments over time. For sellers, LTOs can unlock deals that might not happen as straight cash sales.

The 24-month LTO at $74,456 shows that buyers are willing to commit to structured payment plans for the right domain. This deal structure is increasingly common for domains in the $25,000 to $250,000 range, where the total price is significant but manageable when spread across monthly payments.

Platforms like Atom.com have made LTO deals more accessible, providing escrow-like protections for both parties and streamlining the payment process.

### Five-Figure Acronym Sales Show Market Depth

The stt.ai deal at $74,456 shows that the .ai market has depth beyond the headline-grabbing six and seven-figure word domain sales. Acronym domains with strong keyword associations are finding buyers at meaningful price points.

This creates an opportunity for investors holding 3-letter .ai domains tied to recognized abbreviations. If the abbreviation connects to an AI technology, a growing market, or a well-funded industry, the domain has legitimate five-figure-and-up potential.

## 3-Letter .ai Domains — Investment Opportunity Analysis

The stt.ai sale raises an obvious question for domain investors: are other 3-letter .ai domains worth acquiring?

### The Investment Thesis

The case for 3-letter .ai domains as investments rests on three pillars.

**Limited supply.** Only 17,576 possible combinations exist. This number never changes.

**Growing demand.** The AI industry is expanding rapidly, and new companies need domain names. As single-word .ai domains become unavailable, 3-letter domains are the next natural choice.

**Price appreciation.** Historical data shows that short .ai domain prices have increased year over year, driven by both industry growth and increased awareness of .ai as a premium extension.

### Which 3-Letter Combinations Are Most Valuable

Not all 3-letter .ai domains are created equal. The most valuable combinations share specific characteristics.

**AI technology acronyms.** Domains that match recognized AI terms command the highest premiums. STT (speech-to-text), NLP (natural language processing), LLM (large language model), TTS (text-to-speech), OCR (optical character recognition), and similar abbreviations top the list.

**Pronounceable combinations.** Letters that can be said smoothly as a sequence are more brandable. Compare "stt" (three clean consonants) to something like "xqz" (awkward to say).

**Common business acronyms.** Combinations that overlap with business terms — CRM, ERP, API — gain value from cross-industry demand, not just AI buyers.

**Vowel-containing combinations.** Three-letter strings that include vowels are generally easier to brand as pseudo-words. Combinations like "rai" or "evo" can function as names rather than pure abbreviations.

<!-- IMAGE: Grid showing categories of valuable 3-letter .ai domains organized by type (AI acronyms, business terms, pronounceable), alt="Grid categorizing valuable 3-letter .ai domains by AI acronyms, business terms, and pronounceable combinations" -->

### Risks and Considerations

Domain investing is not risk-free, and 3-letter .ai domains carry specific considerations.

**Renewal costs.** .ai domains have higher renewal fees than .com domains. Annual renewal costs typically run $50 to $100, which adds up if you are holding a portfolio of domains for years before selling.

**Anguilla dependency.** The .ai extension is technically the country-code TLD for Anguilla. While the Anguillan government has embraced the commercial value of .ai and actively promotes registrations, the extension is ultimately governed by a small island nation. Policy changes are unlikely but not impossible.

**Liquidity.** Domain sales are not like stock trades. Finding a buyer at your target price can take months or years. Most domains never sell at all. The stt.ai deal at $74,456 is a strong result, but not every 3-letter .ai domain will achieve similar outcomes.

**Market cycles.** AI hype could cool. If interest in artificial intelligence declines (unlikely in the near term but possible long-term), demand for .ai domains could soften.
